pages.raw_text:
The various classes and depart
ments of the Woman's club; which
were scheduled to be held this
week, have been postponed until
next week, when the active seasons
work will be taken up. The classes
will meet as follows:


Monday, October 10-Music at
2:30 p. M, under the direction of
Miss May Carolyn Williams, chair
man and teacher, at her home, 724
Cleveland avenue. Biographical re-
view of great composers and cur
rent topics in music.


Tuesday, October 11-At 2:30
p. m. a general meeting Of all
women who are interested in Arts
and Crafts. Glasses in French will
meet at the home of Mrs. Belle
Deming, 1908 Eleventh avenue.
First year class from 2:45 to $3.50
p. M: second year class from $3.50
to AIS p. M, Professor lie Gras, in
structor. Miss Kate B. Reed, chair
man,
